frameworks.name: deep neural network representations
frameworks.one_line: analyzing sentence representations in a deep NLP model

frameworks.name: compositional / systematic representation
frameworks.one_line: diagnostic tests for abstract composable structure in learned representations

frameworks.name: human cognitive analogues (zero-shot reasoning, belief bias)
frameworks.one_line: drawing parallels between model behavior and human heuristics

core_question: Do learned sentence representations in deep NLP networks exhibit abstract composable structure similar to humans, or rely on heuristics?
key_assumptions: diagnostic test sets can probe degree of compositional structure
training distribution shapes which strategies are learned
model-human parallels are informative for both cognition and AI
manipulations: diagnostic test dataset probing abstract composable structure
augmentations applied to the training set
key_findings: representations lack systematicity and rely on heuristic strategies
training distribution shifts alter learned heuristics
models can generalize abstract rules zero-shot under certain conditions
generalization failures parallel human judgment errors like belief bias
interpretations: model-human parallels suggest shared explanations for psychological phenomena
findings inform best strategies for building human-like language understanding
